calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: George McNitt appeared by zoom for and with Jerry Kopel by zoom.

The Court noted that this matter is on for hearing for Mr. McNitt to argue his position regarding the requirement to deposit estate funds into federally insured financial institutions. Mr. McNitt stated that he wants to be educated on this issue, that he does not see it as mandatory, referred to Probate Code 10533(a) and stated that he does not think there is a mandate. The Court asked where's the authority to put money in uninsured accounts. Mr. McNitt stated that there is none. The Court stated that this is the limitation that is set by the Legislature, referred to section 46 of the probate code, stated that it will not allow funds to be placed in financial institutions that are not federally insured and that having uninsured accounts is an incredible risk. The Court also stated that in the future if Mr. McNitt counsels his clients not to open federally insured accounts, it will require full bond, spoke of and named multiple credit unions that have been liquidated, stated that it does not know why anyone would counsel their Clients to take that risk, and spoke of sweep accounts that will move funds to ensure that they are all insured. The First and Final Report of Administrator on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.
